ogTitle: McLeans Ridges vs McMahons Point: Climate Safety
ogDescription: Data-driven comparison of climate risk in McLeans Ridges and McMahons Point.
heading: McLeans Ridges vs McMahons Point — Which is Safer?
intro: Compare climate risk between McLeans Ridges, New South Wales and McMahons Point, New South Wales. Our analysis uses data from BOM, CSIRO, and state hazard authorities to score each suburb across 6 climate hazards.
verdict: McLeans Ridges has a lower overall climate risk score (35/100) compared to McMahons Point (37/100). We recommend getting an address-level report for properties in both suburbs before making a purchase decision.

faq.question: Is McLeans Ridges or McMahons Point safer from flooding?
faq.answer: McLeans Ridges has a flood risk score of 40/100, while McMahons Point scores 42/100. A lower score indicates lower flood risk. Get an address-level flood risk report for a specific property.

faq.question: Which suburb has lower bushfire risk?
faq.answer: McLeans Ridges scores 35/100 for bushfire risk. McMahons Point scores 17/100. Fire risk depends on proximity to vegetation and local fire history.

faq.question: Should I buy in McLeans Ridges or McMahons Point?
faq.answer: Both suburbs have climate considerations. McLeans Ridges has a lower overall climate risk score. We recommend getting a full ClimateNest report for any specific property you're considering, which includes 8 hazard scores, insurance projections, and actionable recommendations.

valueImpact.description: Properties in this area may face a 1.1% climate value drag over time due to rising insurance costs and climate adaptation expenses.
